---
title: "Week 10"
description: "Nonparametric Tests and Correlation and Simple Linear Regression"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
---

```{=html}
<!--
Think of the weekly page as answering just five student questions:
    1.  What are we doing this week?
    2.  What do I need to read/watch before class?
    3.  What materials should I have open during class?
    4.  What is due (and when)?
    5.  Where do I go if I want to review later?
    -->
```

## Announcements

-   **HW 8** is released **Monday, March 9** and is due **Sunday, March 15 at 11:00 PM**

## Overview

This week we extend our toolkit to handle situations where the standard parametric assumptions break down, and then introduce a new class of questions: what happens when both variables are continuous?

Monday covers nonparametric tests — rank-based alternatives to the t-test and ANOVA that are useful when data are skewed, samples are small, or outcomes are ordinal. If time permits, we will also begin R Module 3 on visual communication with ggplot2. Wednesday shifts to correlation and simple linear regression, where the goal is no longer comparing groups but understanding the relationship between two continuous variables.

By the end of the week, you should be able to:

-   explain when and why nonparametric tests are preferred over parametric alternatives,
-   describe the core idea behind rank-based tests,
-   apply the Wilcoxon signed-rank test to paired or single-sample data,
-   apply the Wilcoxon rank-sum test to two independent samples,
-   apply the Kruskal-Wallis test to compare three or more groups,
-   choose the appropriate nonparametric test for a given study design,
-   calculate and interpret Pearson and Spearman correlation coefficients,
-   identify the components of the simple linear regression model,
-   run a simple linear regression in R and interpret the slope and intercept,
-   check the LINE assumptions using diagnostic plots,
-   interpret $R^2$ as a measure of model fit, and
-   conduct and interpret inference for the population slope $\beta_1$.

## Readings (before class)

-   No textbook reading for Monday (nonparametric tests are not covered in the textbook — see the supplementary references on the slides)
-   Textbook sections 1.6, 6.1–6.4 (before Wednesday)

## Materials

### Monday — Nonparametric Tests

-   **Topics**
    -   When and why to use nonparametric tests
    -   The rank-based approach
    -   Wilcoxon signed-rank test (paired and single-sample data)
    -   Wilcoxon rank-sum test (two independent groups)
    -   Kruskal-Wallis test (3+ groups) and post-hoc comparisons
    -   *If time permits:* R Module 3 — Visual Communication with ggplot2
-   **Slides**
    -   Nonparametric Tests ([HTML](/lessons/20_nonparametric/20_nonparametric.qmd) | [PDF](/lessons/20_nonparametric/20_nonparametric.pdf) | [OneDrive](https://ohsuitg-my.sharepoint.com/:f:/g/personal/latour_ohsu_edu/IgCOtOYcIrovSKF4qK2E2G-dAYxn57PU_3BBTsgcOoepe1Q?e=eC1m6l))
    -   R Module 3: Visual Communication with ggplot2 ([HTML](/lessons/20_nonparametric/r_module3_slides.qmd) | [PDF](/lessons/20_nonparametric/r_module3_slides.pdf) | [OneDrive](https://ohsuitg-my.sharepoint.com/:f:/g/personal/latour_ohsu_edu/IgCOtOYcIrovSKF4qK2E2G-dAYxn57PU_3BBTsgcOoepe1Q?e=eC1m6l))
-   **Code along**
    -   [Download R Module 3: Visual Communication with ggplot2 (.qmd)](https://ohsuitg-my.sharepoint.com/:u:/g/personal/latour_ohsu_edu/IQC0uyj2CNDLQZ-yM0HyAYCAAUSV5q-CjGfAoHOo2bfdERc?e=U2vIeB)
-   **Recording**
    -   Coming soon
-   **Muddy points**
    -   Coming soon

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Wednesday — Correlation and Simple Linear Regression

-   **Topics**
    -   Pearson and Spearman correlation
    -   The simple linear regression model: slope, intercept, and residuals
    -   Fitting and interpreting `lm()` in R
    -   LINE assumptions and diagnostic plots
    -   $R^2$ and inference for slope $\beta_1$
    -   Prediction: confidence intervals vs. prediction intervals
-   **Slides**
    -   Correlation and Simple Linear Regression (HTML | PDF | OneDrive)
-   **Recording**
    -   Coming soon
-   **Muddy points**
    -   Coming soon

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Homework

-   **HW 8**
    -   Released **Monday, March 9**
    -   Due **Sunday, March 15 at 11:00 PM**
    -   Covers:
        -   ANOVA and post-hoc testing
        -   Nonparametric alternatives (Wilcoxon rank-sum, Kruskal-Wallis)
    -   Data files needed: `knee_rehab.csv` (available in the [data folder on OneDrive](https://ohsuitg-my.sharepoint.com/:f:/g/personal/latour_ohsu_edu/IgBmKLHwwBXcRpYnSfP7XNYfAUi8RENlx_g8XH7VGjPtSuk?e=cvGlF4))
    -   [View HW 8 instructions](/homework/HW08/HW08.qmd)

-   See the full list of assignments on the [Homework](/homework.qmd) page.

## Post-class check-in (attendance)

-   Please complete the post-class check-in after each class meeting.
-   Used for attendance credit and to identify topics that need clarification.
-   Link: [Post-class check-in](https://forms.gle/AMX1S1vCGEEfmwLY6)

## Looking ahead

-   **Week 11**:
    -   Monday — Finals review (Lesson 19)
    -   **Final exam opens Monday, March 16 at 3:00 PM**
    -   Wednesday — Optional review and questions session (no new material); textbook section 7.1 recommended reading
    -   **Final exam closes Friday, March 20 at 5:00 PM**
    -   The final is cumulative with emphasis on Lessons 10–19
